title: Earnings call: BlackBerry reports solid Q1 with focus on IoT and cybersecurity
text: BlackBerry reported solid Q1 2025 fiscal results with focus on IoT and cybersecurity divisions. In Q1, the company reported total revenue of $144 million, with its cybersecurity and IoT divisions collectively contributing 96% of total revenue. IoT revenue surged 18% year-over-year to $53 million, driven by QNX's 8.1% revenue increase to $57.5 million. IoT gross margin reached 81%. The Cybersecurity segment reported $85 million in revenue, with Cybersecurity ARR of $285 million and a dollar-based net retention rate of 87%. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $7 million. Total cash and investments reached $283 million. Operating cash usage was sequentially flat at $15 million, while free cash usage decreased to $16 million. BlackBerry maintained a revenue forecast of $508-538 million for 2026.
